title: Tord Gamb
content:

{{Quote|While the apprehension of this man is of importance to the security of the New Republic, it occurs to me that a man of Tord's abilities could be an asset to our cause.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="WBC" />}}
'''Tord Gamb''' was a con-artist and [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] who was born on [[Port Evokk]]. He was known to have engaged in confidence schemes in the [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]], [[Bimmisaari/Legends|Bimmisaari]], [[Maphus Tria]], [[Algarra II]], and [[Asmall]] systems. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] placed a 25,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head. [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] was willing to negotiate with Gamb, offering a job within the Republic, in exchange for his information finding skills.

==Biography==
Tord Gamb was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Port Evokk]] in [[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]]. A [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]] with a history of frauds, cons and deceptions, Gamb was relatively quiet until the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Posing as a [[free-trader]] with supplies needed by the [[Sluis Van Shipyards]], Gamb gained access to the installation and proceeded to con numerous individuals out of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] totaling several hundred thousand.<ref name="WBC" />

Gamb proceeded to pull similar operations on the [[Planet/Legends|planets]] [[Bimmisaari/Legends|Bimmisaari]], [[Maphus Tria]], and [[Algarra II]]. It was also believed that Gamb was in possession of detailed information regarding the New Republic's defense net around the manufacturing planet of [[Asmall]]. If the information were to reach the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the world could have fallen. The New Republic posted a 25,000 credit reward for Gamb's capture on the charges of [[Thief/Legends|theft]], larceny, fraud, [[Spy/Legends|espionage]], and theft of [[Computer/Legends|computer]] data. Despite the danger Gamb's possession of the Asmall defense data posed to the New Republic, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] believed the gambler could be an asset due to his underworld contacts. Agents were authorized to approach Gamb and offer him a deal; in exchange for his services to the New Republic, all charges could be dropped.<ref name="WBC" />

==Personality and traits==
Gamb was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. He as a consummate gambler and con man, with a skill for bargaining, persuading other individuals, and conducting investigations.<ref name="WBC" />

==Skills and abilities==
Tord Gamb was trained to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], although did not carry one. A thief and con artist, Gamb was also a skilled pick pocket. He was knowledgeable about [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], cultures, languages, and [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]], and had an understanding of [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]] procedure and business practices. Gamb had valuable underworld contacts, and could determine the value of items.<ref name="WBC" />

Gamb was a skilled [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], able to navigate space [[Transport/Legends|transports]] through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]]. He was also proficient with the weapon and [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] systems on small vessels, and was trained in their repair. Gamb's technical skills also included computer programming, [[Droid/Legends|droid]] programming and repair, and bypassing security systems. He could also operate [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles, and ride domestic beasts. Gamb was also trained in stealth tactics, as well as demolition techniques.<ref name="WBC" />

==Equipment==
Tord Gamb owned and operated a stock [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]]. He also had a [[pocket computer]] with three pre-programmed [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumps, and a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tord Gamb was profiled in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], published in [[1993]] by [[West End Games]] for use with the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The image was provided by [[Mike Vilardi]].
